free_text: Yields of the O-18(P,A)N-15 and O-18(P,N)F-18 Reactions
For Protons of 800 KeV to 3500 KeV.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
The Target was Produced by Oxidizing a 0.005-Mil Nickel
Foil in an Atmosphere Enriched in O-18.
O-18(P,A)N-15 and O-18(P,N)F-18 Resonances were
Observed. The Values of Resonance Width at Half-Maximum
Were Observed.
.For Proton Energies Below 900 KeV a 0.35-Mil
Aluminum Counter Window was Used. For Higher Energies,
Where the Protons Entered the Counter, Various Window
Thickness and Counter Fillings were Chosen So That the
Pulses from the Alpha Particles and Protons were Easily
Separated by the 10-Channel Pulse-Height Analyzer.
The Alpha-Particle Pulses were Identified As Such by
Comparison with Pulses from Polonium Alpha-Particles
Under Conditions Where the Two would Lose the Same
Energy in the Counter.
.By CAJAD.
/by Authors/.The Yield Scale For the Neutron Production
Is Arbitrary Since the Sensitivity of the Neutron
Detector was not Known.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
/by Authors/.Data on Several of the Peaks in the Alpha-
Particle Yield were Repeated Two or Three Times Over
A Period of Three Months.The Absolute Yield was Found
To be Reproducible to Within the Counting Statistics,As
Indicated by the Vertical Extent of Some of the Data
Points on the Graphs and the Energy Scale was
Reproducible to Within +-0.1%.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
